analysis.consistency: The feature aligns well with the established flavor and mechanics of a Rogue class feature, specifically related to stealth, secrecy, and underworld knowledge.
analysis.quality: The description is high quality, clearly explaining both the linguistic (dialect/jargon) and non-linguistic (signs/symbols) aspects of Thieves' Cant. The mechanical component regarding communication time (four times longer) is explicitly stated.

mechanics.description: Grants proficiency in a secret dialect/jargon.
mechanics.effect: Allows coded communication understood only by others who know it.

mechanics.description: Conveying a message takes four times longer than plain speech.
mechanics.effect: A balance against the advantage of secrecy; slow communication time.

mechanics.description: Grants understanding of non-verbal cues related to dangers, guild territory, loot, safe houses, and marks.
mechanics.effect: Provides passive situational awareness and scouting information relevant to criminal activity.

analysis.notes: This is a classic Rogue feature. It's well-defined, non-combat in nature, and strongly supports the archetype's theme. The time penalty adds a reasonable balancing constraint to the linguistic advantage.
